edges.description: Altered ARHGAP36-region regulation affects follicular compartments central to BDCS ectodermal findings.
Follicular and basal-cell-tumor tissue abnormalities intersect with ciliary and Hedgehog-pathway mechanisms described in inherited BCC studies.
edges.target: Ciliary and Hedgehog pathway abnormalities in inherited basal-cell-cancer biology

edges.description: Ciliary and Hedgehog-pathway abnormalities support the combined ectodermal and basal-cell-carcinoma phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0000889 | Abnormality of the clavicle | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0001167 | Abnormality of finger | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0002671 | Basal cell carcinoma | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Early onset basal cell carcinoma: Consider Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome is a rare genetic condition characterised by basal cell carcinomas, follicular atrophoderma and hypotrichosis.

nodes.description: Mechanistic studies in BDCS-associated and inherited basal-cell-cancer contexts show shortened primary cilia and Hedgehog pathway activation, offering a plausible route from follicular dysregulation to basal-cell carcinoma susceptibility.
nodes.id: Ciliary and Hedgehog pathway abnormalities in inherited basal-cell-cancer biology

evidence.reference_title: ARP-T1-associated Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome is an inherited basal cell cancer with ciliary defects characteristic of ciliopathies.
evidence.snippet: Tissue samples from ARP-T1-associated BDCS patients have reduced ciliary length.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in ACTRT1 and its enhancer RNA elements lead to aberrant activation of Hedgehog signaling in inherited and sporadic basal cell carcinomas.
evidence.snippet: loss of ARP-T1 led to activation of the Hedgehog pathway in individuals with BDCS.

evidence.reference_title: Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0002208 | Coarse hair | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0100777 | Exostoses | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: A Scottish family with Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome: follicular atrophoderma, congenital hypotrichosis, and basal cell carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Follicular atrophoderma (FA) are follicular funnel shaped depressions, "ice pick marks", seen most commonly on the dorsum of the hands.

nodes.description: The downstream syndrome combines congenital hypotrichosis and hair-shaft abnormalities, milia, hypohidrosis, follicular atrophoderma, and early-onset basal cell carcinomas.
nodes.id: Follicular ectodermal phenotype and basal cell carcinoma susceptibility

evidence.reference_title: Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome: review of clinical and molecular aspects.
evidence.snippet: the classical triad of basal cell carcinoma, follicular atrophoderma, and hypotrichosis

evidence.reference_title: Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome in a 1-year-old boy and his mother.
evidence.snippet: characterized by follicular atrophoderma, multiple milia, congenital hypotrichosis, hypohidrosis and basal cell malformations

nodes.description: BDCS affects the hair follicle compartment, producing congenital hypotrichosis, hair-shaft abnormalities, follicular atrophoderma, and related ectodermal signs.

evidence.reference_title: Germline intergenic duplications at Xq26.1 underlie Bazex-Dupré-Christol basal cell carcinoma susceptibility syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We detected ARHGAP36 expression near the control hair follicular stem cell compartment, and found increased ARHGAP36 levels in hair follicles in telogen, in BCCs and in trichoepitheliomas from patients with BDCS.

evidence.reference_title: A Scottish family with Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome: follicular atrophoderma, congenital hypotrichosis, and basal cell carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Bazex-Dupre-Christol syndrome (BDCS) is an X linked dominant disorder of the hair follicle characterised by follicular atrophoderma, multiple basal cell carcinomas, hypotrichosis, milia, and localised hypohidrosis.

evidence.reference_title: Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome in a 1-year-old boy and his mother.
evidence.snippet: Hypohidrosis was observed on his trunk and head.

evidence.reference_title: Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0100720 | Hypoplasia of the ear cartilage | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Basal cell carcinomas arising in BDCS require standard lesion-directed BCC management, with surgical removal used for most lesions after risk stratification.
nodes.id: Lesion-directed surgical removal of basal cell carcinomas

evidence.reference_title: S2k guideline basal cell carcinoma of the skin (update 2023)
evidence.snippet: Surgical removal remains the treatment of first choice in most cases.

nodes.description: Germline noncoding tandem duplications at Xq26.1 alter a regulatory region flanked by ARHGAP36 and IGSF1, most likely causing abnormal ARHGAP36 expression in follicular and basal-cell-tumor-relevant tissues.

evidence.reference_title: Germline intergenic duplications at Xq26.1 underlie Bazex-Dupré-Christol basal cell carcinoma susceptibility syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The Xq26.1 BDCS duplications likely dysregulate ARHGAP36, the flanking centromeric gene.

meta.association: Noncoding tandem duplications at Xq26.1 most likely dysregulate ARHGAP36

evidence.reference_title: Germline intergenic duplications at Xq26.1 underlie Bazex-Dupré-Christol basal cell carcinoma susceptibility syndrome.
evidence.snippet: In eight families with BDCS, we identified overlapping 18-135-kb duplications (six inherited and two de novo) at Xq26.1, flanked by ARHGAP36 and IGSF1.

evidence.reference_title: Germline intergenic duplications at Xq26.1 underlie Bazex-Dupré-Christol basal cell carcinoma susceptibility syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Noncoding Xq26.1 duplications cause BDCS.

evidence.reference_title: Germline intergenic duplications at Xq26.1 underlie Bazex-Dupré-Christol basal cell carcinoma susceptibility syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The BDCS duplications most likely lead to dysregulation of ARHGAP36.

evidence.reference_title: Germline intergenic duplications at Xq26.1 underlie Bazex-Dupré-Christol basal cell carcinoma susceptibility syndrome.
evidence.snippet: ACTRT1 loss-of-function variants are unlikely to cause BDCS.
